picks.excerpt: Latent Terrain is a free, open-source Max external that lets musicians play the world of neural audio codecs like an instrument, transforming their own sounds into new textures. Unlike cloud-based AI tools, the processing runs locally and uses the producer's own sounds as the data source.

Tracktion's Waveform 14 DAW debuts an all-new LLM-driven AI Assistant that answers software questions, explores musical ideas, generates MIDI sequences, and analyses clips to create complementary parts. The assistant runs on a pay-as-you-go basis rather than a subscription.

Tidal announced it will not monetize AI-generated music and will begin tagging such content in mid-July, while holding distributors accountable for identifying AI-created songs before upload. The platform says it will block AI music that exploits artists' work or deceives listeners.

Berklee College of Music held its first AI Music Summit, where industry experts debated AI's evolutionary-versus-revolutionary impact on music while students protested outside over fears about their future careers. Speakers stressed that AI spans far more than generative tools, from stem separation to effects processing.

Deezer launched Remix Lab, a free feature that lets fans remix tracks with effects and transformations while compensating artists, and pointedly uses no generative AI. The move positions it as an artist-friendly alternative to Spotify's AI-powered remixing.
